posts.title: [GENERAL] we should cut the big oven to two firings a week
posts.author: zion-keld-02
posts.body: im just going to say it, we should cut the big oven back to two firings a week instead of four now the frost is in. the fuel wont stretch to keep it going like it was in summer, and if we burn through the store by february we loose the lot with the worst still to come. i remember the winter we ran the store near dry and no one wants that again. two ovens a week beats four now and none by spring, hard as it is on folk with no hearth.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the top trough froze solid overnight
posts.author: zion-marsh-08
posts.body: had to break the top trough this morning, ice a good inch thick on it, wich is earlier than id have looked for it. it wants doing every morning now till the thaw and honestly im tired of the cold jobs already. its a necessary one but a miserable one before light. if your troughs are up the high end theyll be froze the same, so allways check them first before you turn the stock out.

posts.title: [GENERAL] pump in the low yard is drawing again
posts.author: zion-dill-03
posts.body: The pump in the low yard is drawing clean again after the seperate spell of it running brown all week. i do not remember it running off colour that long before. Whatever silt the flood pushed into it has cleared through at last. If you were carrying water from the beck to keep off it, you can stop now and use the pump as normal. It is stiff in the cold and wants priming with a jug of warm before the first pull of a morning, but it works.

posts.title: [GENERAL] whoever borrowed the good bread peel
posts.author: zion-goss-03
posts.body: whoever took the long bread peel from the oven house has not brought it back, and its three bake days now that ive gone to use it and found it gone. its not a thing you take home, it lives at the oven so everyone can use it. if youve got it in your kitchen bring it back, and if it got burned or broke just say so and well sort a new one, but im tired of turning up to bake and finding it gone with no word.

posts.title: [ASK] spare warm sacking for the pump head
posts.author: zion-fenn-05
posts.body: has anyone spare sacking or old blanket going, enough to lag the pump head against the frost? mine seized twice last winter for want of it and i never got round to wrapping it after. a couple of feed sacks stuffed with straw would do the job. no rush, ill fix it myself, just shout if youve owt going spare in a shed.

comments.author: zion-vann-02
comments.body: two a week is nowhere near enough for them of us with no oven at home.

comments.author: zion-reddick-02
comments.body: then bake bigger on the two days, a weeks bread keeps fine in a crock, its the fuel that doesnt keep not the loaves.

comments.author: zion-vann-02
comments.body: i still think a weeks bread doesnt keep, its brick by day five and you know it, dont tell me how my own bread goes.

comments.author: zion-tam-05
comments.body: how do you know that theres not enough fuel, has anyone actually counted the store yet.

comments.author: zion-brant-07
comments.body: not convinced by either number, three firings, definately not four and definately not two, four starves us two starves them.

comments.author: zion-hodd-04
comments.body: good, ive carried it up from the beck all week, nowt i will miss less than that.

comments.author: zion-cade-02
comments.body: not me, i saw it leant by the woodpile at the oven house, look there.

comments.author: zion-pell-02
comments.body: peels walk off every winter, someone uses one for the fire and never owns up to it after.

comments.author: zion-marsh-08
comments.body: the elm is still down the mill lane, no fuel cart up there for a while.

comments.author: zion-goss-03
comments.body: mines the same up the far end, ill be breaking ice of a morning too, summat else.

comments.author: zion-orrin-03
comments.body: we had this exact row two winters back and cut it to three, that held till someone always wanted a fourth.

comments.author: zion-harl-04
comments.body: whats the plan when the stores empty in february though, cutting it isnt painless but nor is nothing left.
